title: Why This $3 Million Buy in a 400-Bond ETF Highlights Long-Term Income Positioning
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Clark Asset Management purchased 205,205 shares of the Invesco BulletShares 2031 Corporate Bond ETF (BSCV) for approximately $3.42 million in Q1 2026, bringing its total position to $16.05 million. The purchase reflects a deliberate bond laddering strategy prioritizing predictable income through investment-grade corporate bonds maturing in 2031, layered on top of a diversified equity foundation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The ETF is highlighted as an attractive investment vehicle with a competitive 4.68% SEC yield, low 0.10% expense ratio, diversified portfolio of ~400 investment-grade bonds, and clear maturity structure. The institutional purchase and strategic positioning suggest confidence in its role within a long-term income strategy.

title: This $8 Million ETF Buy Extends a Bond Ladder Spanning 2026 Through 2031
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Indiana-based Kirr Marbach acquired 466,959 shares of the Invesco BulletShares 2031 Corporate Bond ETF (BSCV) worth approximately $7.80 million in Q4, extending its bond ladder strategy across maturities from 2026 through 2031. The move provides predictable cash flow and flexibility while maintaining investment-grade corporate bond exposure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The ETF is being actively acquired as part of a strategic bond ladder extension, demonstrating investor confidence in its investment-grade corporate bond portfolio, competitive yield (4.7%), and transparent structure for managing maturity risk.
Listed as a top holding in Kirr Marbach's portfolio (7.1% of AUM), but the article provides no specific commentary on the company's performance or outlook.
